author: mushow0x
content:
hey there, while reviewing this PR. I thought: 

*What if someone wants to waste 1 ETH every 8192 epochs*?

That could prevent the builder from withdrawing as it would reset the timer every time. 

It's kind of a grief attack, but imagine a builder has 4000 ETH and wants to withdraw it. They initiate their exit, the timer starts ticking... and right before the sweep would pay them out, anyone (no signature needed on a top-up) sends a 1 ETH deposit to their pubkey. 

The reset fires, `withdrawable_epoch` jumps another 8192 epochs into the future, and the 4000 ETH stays locked. 

Repeat once a month and the builder never gets their funds out. ~1 ETH/month to freeze an arbitrary builder indefinitely.

It's donated to the victim, but the victim still can't withdraw

author: mushow0x
content: Curious to hear your thoughts on that.

author: jtraglia
content: Hey <@1515072075214229704> 👋 good feedback! Yes, this is possible. I'm not sure if this can be fixed without adjusting the min withdrawal delay, which we intentionally made a very high value. My suggestion then would be to advise builders not to exit their builders with a large balance, but rather continue being a builder until the balance is small. An attacker would less likely to perform this attack to prevent a builder from getting 1 ETH back; I wish an attacker would give me 1 ETH a month in that case 😄 maybe I should add a note in builder.md somewhere?

author: potuz
content: This is a good thing to document. Nice one <@1515072075214229704> . But yeah I think we need to document this and not act on it

author: mushow0x
content:
I was thinking checking `balance == 0`, but I'm not sure if it would work. I'd have to try and create tests. 

Having something like:

```r
 if builder.withdrawable_epoch != FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH and builder.balance == 0:
    epoch = get_current_epoch(state)
    builder.withdrawable_epoch = epoch + MIN_BUILDER_WITHDRAWABILITY_DELAY
```

And then do the 

```r
builder.balance += amount
```

Afterwards.

author: mushow0x
content: Considering that the PR is to prevent spam-sweep, and if just sweep'd then balance is set to 0.

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah that might be better. The builder would get their ETH back, but the sweep attack wouldn't be possible.

author: jtraglia
content: I'll give it some more thought, but I think this could work.

author: brechy
content: I believe the balance check could work as well, happy to review!

author: jtraglia
content: <@755590043632140352> <@1019999229151821936> why are y'all working?! Please ignore this & enjoy the football game.

author: nflaig
content: what about we get rid of `MIN_BUILDER_WITHDRAWABILITY_DELAY` entirely, with 8282 this should be safe?

author: nflaig
content: you can only have 2 builder exits per payload without bumping the base fee

author: nflaig
content: but https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5359#discussion_r3442158818 might be an issue then

author: jtraglia
content: I believe there's still value in keeping it. If we remove `MIN_BUILDER_WITHDRAWABILITY_DELAY` and remove this "if exited, reset the withdrawable epoch" block of code, wouldn't that open up the original issue again? https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5373

author: nflaig
content: also Potuz needs to explain this, I don't think you can do this, it would be really expensive to fill up the exits each payload

author: nflaig
content: we probably still need a short delay, so basically there is an issue if the topup happens between the sweep and the builder actually being exited (ie. removable/reusable from the state), right?

author: potuz
content: I do not think it's possible to spam entirely the queue with 8282 exponential fix in the block So. the delay can be lowered, however, it is still better IMO to keep this number relatively high to discourage arbitrary rotation

author: nflaig
content:
> it is still better IMO to keep this number relatively high to discourage arbitrary rotation
 agree that is good to have

author: jtraglia
content: We should do the math on what value `MIN_BUILDER_WITHDRAWABILITY_DELAY` can be lowered to now.
